title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Guided Terminal Studio
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — On a workshop host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

content.text: On a workshop host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Shell Terminal Kernel condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: The Open Group Base Specifications Issue 8 — Controls portable shell, utility, process, file, environment, and system-interface semantics.

hints: Name the Shell Terminal Kernel precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: On a workshop host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself.

options.text: The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself. This does not resolve commit to the mission model.

For commit to the mission model, apply Shell Terminal Kernel without its stated preconditions, omit the a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Shell Terminal Kernel for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.
State the Shell Terminal Kernel preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
